cards.front: DC equivalent circuit rule for capacitors?
cards.back: All capacitors → open circuits at DC. Remove coupling caps (C1, C2), bypass caps (CE), AC source, and load.
Find Thevenin: VTh = R2·VCC/(R1+R2), RTh = R1||R2.
KVL base loop: VTh = IB·RTh + VBE + (β+1)IB·RE → solve for IB.
IC = βIB, IE = (β+1)IB. Then KVL collector: VCE = VCC − IC·RC − IE·RE.
Check VCE > VCE(sat) ≈ 0.2V. If VCE is too small, transistor is saturated — redo with saturation model.
RE = VE/IE. The emitter voltage VE appears directly across RE (emitter to ground).
RC = (VCC − VC)/IC. The collector resistor drops VCC − VC with current IC flowing through it.
VB = VE + VBE. The base is always one BE junction drop above the emitter.
When β >> 1 (which is usually true, β=100-200). Then IE = (β+1)IB ≈ βIB = IC.
